evaluationFactors: A. Responsiveness to the RFP (20 points maximum): 1. Requested information included and thoroughness of response. 2. Understanding of the project and ability to deliver as exhibited in the Executive Summary. 3. Technical Aspects. 4. Risk Profile (e.g. exceptions request).

B. Staffing Plan (20 points maximum): 1. Qualifications of personnel adequate for requirement. 2. Description of the Contractor's history, mission, location(s), date founded, size, key leadership and relevant certifications. 3. List of names and titles of key managers who will be assigned to this project. 4. Description of the company and/or key managers' experience and knowledge of local and federal regulations related to CDBG, ESG, and HOME. 5. Method of Quality Assurance, Compliance with applicable laws, Internal Control Procedures. 6. Availability/Geographical location of personnel for required tasks. 7. Customer Service/Capacity to provide timely response to City and subrecipients.

C. Contractor's Capability to provide the services and expertise and Past Performance (35 points maximum): 1. Relevant experience of the Contractor and subcontractors in working with governmental agencies. 2. Fulfillment of City of San Diego IT requirements. 3. Specific experience with Federal reporting and ability to integrate to Federal reporting systems. 4. Other applicable pertinent experience. 5. Past/Prior Performance including timely execution of go-live. 6. Provide three (3) examples of successfully delivered SaaS/IT projects. 7. List of government contracts performed that are related to SaaS/IT within the past five (5) years. 8. Capacity/Capability to meet The City of San Diego needs in a timely manner. 9. Reference checks.
D. Price (10 points maximum): Lowest total estimated contract price receives maximum points; others scored using formula (1 - (contract price - lowest price) / lowest price) x maximum points.

E. Interview/Oral Presentation (15 points maximum): 1. Usability of Software. 2. Support Model. 3. Real Time Operation including demonstration of the software meeting key requirements. 4. Thoroughness and Clarity of Presentation.
F. Participation by Small Local Business Enterprise (SLBE) or Emerging Local Business Enterprise (ELBE) Firms (up to 12 additional percentage points applied to final score).
overview: The City of San Diego, through its Economic Development Department (EDD), is soliciting proposals (Solicitation Number 10090460-26-S) for a Grants Management System (GMS) delivered as a vendor-hosted, subscription-based Software as a Service (SaaS) solution. The system is needed to streamline and enhance the City's ability to manage and track local and federal grants and funding programs, including Business Districts (MADs, PBIDs, BIDs), Community Development Programs (HUD-funded CDBG, ESG, HOME), and Capacity Building programs, replacing current fragmented tools such as Excel, SharePoint, SeamlessDocs, and a Salesforce-backed reporting system. Success is defined as a comprehensive, turn-key solution that provides an intuitive user interface for internal and external users, seamless end-to-end grant lifecycle management from application through closeout, integrated reporting and dashboard tools, compliance with state and federal regulations (including HUD), integration with City systems (SAP, OKTA, ArcGIS, IDIS, MS365, Adobe), and a public-facing portal for grant recipients to manage applications, agreements, reimbursements, and reporting. Key constraints include a desired go-live date of July 1, 2026, a base contract term of three years with two optional one-year extensions (not to exceed five years total), ADA/WCAG 2.1 Level AA and Section 508 compliance, 99.9% uptime availability with service credit remedies, City of San Diego IT Standards and Data Security Standards compliance, Cyber Liability insurance of at least $1,000,000 per occurrence, and proposals due by March 16, 2026 at 2:00 p.m. The evaluation is based on a 112-point scale including Responsiveness to RFP (20 points), Staffing Plan (20 points), Contractor Capability and Past Performance (35 points), Price (10 points), Interview/Oral Presentation (15 points), and SLBE/ELBE participation (up to 12 bonus points).

requirements.requirement: Provide a vendor-hosted, subscription-based SaaS Grants Management System (GMS) solution
Application Intake & Management module supporting multi-stage workflows including RFQ and RFP phases with concurrent multi-user access from the same organization
Public-facing portal for external grant recipients to manage information, submit applications, execute agreements, submit reimbursement requests, and provide outcomes data including grant closeout
Support multiple intake processes for separate grant programs with configurable grant application templates that differ between programs and grant cycles
Multi-step internal and external review workflow with scoring rubrics, ability to leave comments, assign reviewers, and automatically tally and average scores

Document Management functionality including document repository, upload of multiple formats (pdf, jpeg, image, video, .docx, .xlsx, .pptx), document library for templates, and records retention per EDD policy

Financial and Billing Functions including monthly reimbursement request processing, expenditure tracking across budget categories, budget adjustments, customizable fiscal review workflows, and invoice generation with SAP/FICA interface capability

Reporting and Compliance functionality including real-time budget-to-actual expenditures, visual analytics (pie charts, bar graphs, trend lines) exportable to PowerPoint, Word, and Adobe, and audit log functionality compliant with Federal/HUD funding guidelines
Integration with ArcGIS, IDIS, SAP, OKTA, and MS365; proposer must indicate which integrations are out-of-the-box and which require customization
Data migration from existing systems including Microsoft SharePoint, SeamlessDocs, and Salesforce-backed programs with indexing and integration of existing content

User Roles & System Administration supporting view-only to full system access, record-type level role designation, in-system communication between internal and external users as part of audit trail, and email notifications (automated and manual)
Provide three system environments: development/configuration, testing/training, and production
99.9% Uptime Availability per calendar month with service credit remedies for failures below threshold
24/7 system availability
Meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility guidelines, Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT), and Section 508 requirements
Comply with City of San Diego Data Security Standards and immediately notify the City's Chief Information Security Officer of any breach
Comply with City of San Diego IT City Standards as detailed in Exhibit F - IT City Standards and Technical Alignment Questionnaire
Understanding of HUD regulations and requirements to ensure Federal requirements are built into the SaaS solution and not customized (CDBG, ESG, HOME)
Cyber Liability Insurance with limits not less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 annual aggregate
Professional Liability (Errors and Omissions) insurance with limits of at least $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate
Warranty the entire solution/system for the duration of the contract terms from the date the City fully accepts it
Identify a single point of contact Project Manager whose name and resume must be submitted; PM cannot be changed without written City approval
Provide organizational chart with all proposed personnel including supervisor level, functional responsibilities, key personnel, percentage of time dedicated to the project
Identify at minimum the Project Manager, Lead Business Analyst(s), Training Manager, and System Lead with titles, roles, expertise, and work location
Provide brief resumes for key personnel including professional career overview, relevant project experience, educational background, and certifications
Provide a staffing plan indicating hours staff may be reached, method of contact, level of effort to come on-site, and availability/geographical location
Provide a draft implementation plan, draft implementation schedule, and draft outline documents as part of the proposal
Provide a project plan using Microsoft Project or equivalent software including milestones, work breakdown structure, and overall project timeline
Provide a configuration document as a project deliverable before the system's final configuration
Provide a testing strategy and plan including test scripts as a project deliverable; City must sign off on final testing plan before executing tests

Provide End-User Training (up to 75 staff), Functional System Administrator Training (up to 10 staff), and External-User Training (up to 200 non-profit professionals) with combination of virtual and on-site training, training documentation, user guides, quick reference guides, and FAQs
Provide detailed system and user documentation including digital searchable technical and user manuals, system implementation documentation, and PDF copies with unlimited downloads of updated copies
Remote Helpdesk Tier One and Tier Two support Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. PT, excluding federal holidays, for both internal and external users
Ongoing support and maintenance including upgrades, bug fixes, patches, and help desk support for the duration of the contract
Application recovery services including hosting infrastructure recovery, application recovery, and offsite data backup storage with periodic testing

Demonstrate capability by providing proof of prior similar work with at least 10 cities, states, or other public agencies, with at least 2 examples from municipalities of similar size and complexity, preferably within the last 5 years

Provide three (3) references from clients with similar scopes of work within the last five (5) years including organization name, contact information, nature of assignment, and business benefits realized
Provide three (3) examples of successfully delivered SaaS/IT projects including project name, description, scope of work, deliverables, and key technical outcomes
Complete Exhibit E - Grant Management System Requirements Matrix with scoring rubric (0-4 scale) for each requirement
Complete Exhibit F - IT City Standards and Technical Alignment Questionnaire

Clearly indicate licensing model (Named User, Concurrent User, Flat Subscription, or volume-based), license transfer capability, volume change flexibility, unused licensing rollover, and overage cost thresholds
Provide copy of all System User software license agreements to be executed by the City
Desired go-live date of July 1, 2026; provide project schedule with key milestones and proposed dates to meet this target
Submit completed and signed Contractor Standards Pledge of Compliance Form
Submit Equal Opportunity Contracting forms including Work Force Report and Contractors Certification of Pending Actions

format: One (1) original hard copy and one (1) electronic copy (thumb drive or CD), or electronic bid via PlanetBids
Paper proposals must be in a sealed envelope with Solicitation Number and Closing Date on lower left-hand corner
E-mailed and faxed proposals will not be accepted
Exhibits D, E, and F are Excel files posted in PlanetBids and must be completed in their entirety
Executive summary limited to one typewritten page
Completed and signed RFP signature page required with most recent addendum acknowledged
attachmentsAndForms: Completed and signed Contract Signature Page (latest Addendum version if addenda issued)
Exceptions requested by proposer with specific proposed alternative language (if any)
Contractor Standards Pledge of Compliance Form
Equal Opportunity Contracting forms including Work Force Report and Contractors Certification of Pending Actions
Grant Management System Requirements Matrix - Exhibit E (Excel)
City of San Diego IT City Standards & Technical Alignment Questionnaire - Exhibit F (Excel)
Redacted version of proposal for California Public Records Act exemptions (if applicable)
Insurance documents (upon Notice of Intent to Award)
Form W-9 (upon Notice of Intent to Award)
Business Tax Certificate (upon Notice of Intent to Award)

requiredDocuments.name: Executive Summary
requiredDocuments.description: Limited to one typewritten page, providing a high-level description of the proposer's ability to meet RFP requirements and reasons proposer believes itself best qualified, demonstrating knowledge and experience with grant management systems satisfying state and federal mandates

requiredDocuments.name: Tab B - Responses to Specifications
requiredDocuments.description: Proposer's complete response to the RFP including technical approach, software performance history, understanding of HUD regulations, sample cases, and all information outlined in Exhibit B Scope of Work

requiredDocuments.name: Tab C - Cost/Price Proposal (Exhibit D)
requiredDocuments.description: Cost proposal in the form and format described in Exhibit D - Price Proposal; failure to provide costs in required format may result in non-responsive determination

requiredDocuments.name: Grant Management System Requirements Matrix - Exhibit E
requiredDocuments.description: Excel file with scoring rubric (0-4 scale) for each GMS requirement indicating capability level from 'Cannot be provided' to 'Out of the box configurable by City staff'

requiredDocuments.name: IT City Standards & Technical Alignment Questionnaire - Exhibit F
requiredDocuments.description: Excel file addressing City of San Diego IT standards and technical alignment requirements

requiredDocuments.name: Staffing Plan and Qualifications Submittal
requiredDocuments.description: Company overview, staffing plan with organizational chart, key personnel summaries (PM, Lead Business Analyst, Training Manager, System Lead), resumes, availability/location, and hours of contact

requiredDocuments.name: Draft Implementation Plan and Schedule
requiredDocuments.description: Draft implementation plan, draft implementation schedule, and project plan using Microsoft Project or equivalent with key milestones and proposed dates targeting July 1, 2026 go-live

requiredDocuments.name: Past Performance and References
requiredDocuments.description: Three (3) references from clients with similar scopes within last 5 years, three (3) examples of successfully delivered SaaS/IT projects, list of government SaaS/IT contracts within past 5 years, and proof of prior work with at least 10 public agencies

requiredDocuments.name: Project Management Approach
requiredDocuments.description: Description of project management methodology, role of all partners, role of City staff including time commitment, team structure with organizational chart, and detailed plan for GMS implementation

requiredDocuments.name: Contractor Standards Pledge of Compliance Form
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Contractor Standards Pledge of Compliance Form as required by the City

requiredDocuments.name: Equal Opportunity Contracting Forms
requiredDocuments.description: Work Force Report and Contractors Certification of Pending Actions

requiredDocuments.name: Completed and Signed Contract Signature Page
requiredDocuments.description: Signed RFP signature page with most recent addendum listed as acknowledgement of all addenda issued

requiredDocuments.name: Redacted Version of Proposal
requiredDocuments.description: Additional redacted version containing all requested redactions of confidential/proprietary information with legal basis for CPRA exemption, if applicable

requiredDocuments.name: Exceptions to Contract Terms
requiredDocuments.description: Written factual or legal justification for any exceptions requested to the Scope of Work, Contract, or Exhibits, with specific proposed alternative or amended language

requiredDocuments.name: System User Software License Agreements
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of all software license agreements the proposer will request the City to execute, covering all software components including third-party, base system, and custom software

requiredDocuments.name: Training Programs and Materials
requiredDocuments.description: Training programs, curriculum, and examples of training materials for end-users (75 staff), system administrators (10 staff), and external users (200 non-profit professionals)

requiredVolumes: Tab A - Submission of Information and Forms
Tab B - Executive Summary and Responses to Specifications

deliverables: Vendor-hosted SaaS Grants Management System (GMS) with public-facing portal
Configuration document prior to final system configuration
Testing strategy and plan with test scripts
Draft implementation plan and implementation schedule
Project management plan with milestones and work breakdown structure (Microsoft Project or equivalent)
End-User Training for up to 75 staff
Functional System Administrator Training for up to 10 staff
External-User Training for up to 200 non-profit professionals
Training documentation, comprehensive user guides, quick reference guides, and FAQs
Digital searchable technical and user manuals
System implementation documentation (installation, configuration, testing, interfaces, data conversion)
Three system environments: development/configuration, testing/training, and production
Data migration from SharePoint, SeamlessDocs, and Salesforce systems
Ongoing support and maintenance including upgrades, bug fixes, and patches
Remote Helpdesk Tier One and Tier Two support services
Application recovery and offsite data backup services
Monthly Uptime Availability calculations and service credit reporting
